input.background: If two studies' results yield $\mathrm{P}<0.001$ and $\mathrm{P}=0.02$, why is it not necessarily true, as noted in section 16.3.6, that the former has found a stronger effect than the latter?
input.question: Why might two clinical trials of different sizes yield the same treatment effect but different P values?

output.answer: Two clinical trials of different sizes may yield the same treatment effect but different P values.
